title: Agentic AI Learning Roadmap
description: A complete, staged path from beginner to expert — programming and LLM foundations, prompt engineering, the agent loop, reasoning and planning, tool use and function calling, memory and RAG, agent architectures and design patterns, multi-agent systems, frameworks, evaluation and observability, safety and security, production AgentOps, advanced agent types and frontier research.

stages.title: Programming & Software Foundations
stages.summary: Programming, APIs and software engineering basics needed to build agents.

nodes.title: Programming Fundamentals
nodes.description: Core programming concepts and Python for agents.

nodes.title: APIs, Async & Networking
nodes.description: HTTP, REST, async programming and working with web APIs.

nodes.title: Software Engineering Practices
nodes.description: Version control, testing, packaging, environments and debugging.

stages.title: AI & LLM Foundations
stages.summary: Machine learning basics and how large language models work.

nodes.title: ML & Deep Learning Basics
nodes.description: Core machine learning and neural network concepts.

nodes.title: How LLMs Work
nodes.description: Transformers, tokenization, training and generation.

nodes.title: Working with LLMs
nodes.description: Calling models, parameters, context windows and limitations.

stages.title: Prompt Engineering for Agents
stages.summary: Designing prompts, structured outputs and reasoning techniques.

nodes.title: Prompting Fundamentals
nodes.description: Prompt anatomy, roles and core prompting patterns.

nodes.title: Structured Outputs & Schemas
nodes.description: JSON mode, schemas and parsing reliable outputs.

nodes.title: Reasoning Prompting Techniques
nodes.description: Chain-of-thought, few-shot and self-consistency prompting.

stages.title: Agent Fundamentals
stages.summary: What agents are, the agent loop and their core components.

nodes.title: What is an AI Agent?
nodes.description: Definitions, autonomy levels and agents vs workflows.

nodes.title: The Agent Loop
nodes.description: Perception, reasoning, action and observation cycles.

nodes.title: Agent Components & Anatomy
nodes.description: Model, instructions, tools, memory and control logic.

stages.title: Reasoning & Planning
stages.summary: How agents reason, plan and correct themselves.

nodes.title: Reasoning Techniques
nodes.description: Chain, tree and graph of thought and reasoning models.

nodes.title: Planning Approaches
nodes.description: Task decomposition, plan generation and re-planning.

nodes.title: Reflection & Self-Correction
nodes.description: Self-critique, reflection loops and error recovery.

stages.title: Tool Use & Function Calling
stages.summary: Giving agents the ability to act through tools and external systems.

nodes.title: Function Calling
nodes.description: Tool schemas, invocation and handling results.

nodes.title: Tool Design & Integration
nodes.description: Designing, building and securing agent tools.

nodes.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
nodes.description: Standardized tool and context integration with MCP.

nodes.title: Code Execution & Computer Use
nodes.description: Code interpreters, sandboxes and computer-use tools.

stages.title: Memory & Knowledge
stages.summary: How agents store, retrieve and manage memory.

nodes.title: Memory Types
nodes.description: Short-term, long-term, episodic and semantic memory.

nodes.title: Embeddings & Vector Stores
nodes.description: Embeddings, vector databases and semantic search.

nodes.title: Memory Management Strategies
nodes.description: Context management, summarization and forgetting.

stages.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Agents
stages.summary: Grounding agents in external knowledge with retrieval.

nodes.title: RAG Fundamentals
nodes.description: Chunking, indexing, retrieval and generation.

nodes.title: Advanced Retrieval
nodes.description: Hybrid search, re-ranking and query transformation.

nodes.title: Agentic RAG
nodes.description: Agents that decide when and how to retrieve.

stages.title: Agent Architectures & Design Patterns
stages.summary: Single-agent patterns and how to structure agent behavior.

nodes.title: Single-Agent Patterns
nodes.description: ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, Reflexion, ReWOO and more.

nodes.title: Workflows vs Agents
nodes.description: Deterministic workflows versus autonomous agents.

nodes.title: Orchestration & Control Patterns
nodes.description: Routing, chaining, parallelization and control flow.

stages.title: Multi-Agent Systems
stages.summary: Coordinating multiple agents to solve complex tasks.

nodes.title: Multi-Agent Fundamentals
nodes.description: Why and when to use multiple agents, roles and topologies.

nodes.title: Collaboration Patterns
nodes.description: Supervisor, hierarchical, debate and team patterns.

nodes.title: Communication & Coordination
nodes.description: Messaging, protocols, shared state and handoffs.

stages.title: Agent Frameworks & Tooling
stages.summary: Popular frameworks and SDKs for building agents.

nodes.title: LangChain & LangGraph
nodes.description: Chains, graphs, state machines and orchestration.

nodes.title: Microsoft AutoGen
nodes.description: Conversational multi-agent framework.

nodes.title: CrewAI
nodes.description: Role-based multi-agent crews and processes.

nodes.title: Other Frameworks & SDKs
nodes.description: OpenAI Agents SDK, LlamaIndex, Semantic Kernel and more.

stages.title: Building Agentic Applications
stages.summary: Turning agents into real applications and products.

nodes.title: Application Patterns
nodes.description: Common shapes of agentic applications.

nodes.title: State & Structured I/O
nodes.description: Managing state, sessions and structured input/output.

nodes.title: Streaming & Agent UX
nodes.description: Streaming, progress, transparency and failure UX.

stages.title: Human-in-the-Loop & Interaction
stages.summary: Keeping humans in control of autonomous agents.

nodes.title: Human-in-the-Loop Patterns
nodes.description: When and how to involve humans in agent loops.

nodes.title: Approvals & Interrupts
nodes.description: Confirmation gates, interrupts and resuming.

stages.summary: Making agents safe, secure and controllable.

nodes.title: Agent Safety
nodes.description: Alignment, control, misbehavior and risk mitigation.

nodes.title: Security Threats
nodes.description: Prompt injection, data exfiltration and tool abuse.

nodes.description: Input/output guardrails, permissions and isolation.

stages.title: Deployment & AgentOps
stages.summary: Running agents reliably and cost-effectively in production.

nodes.title: Deployment Patterns
nodes.description: Serving agents, APIs, background jobs and scheduling.

nodes.title: Scaling & Performance
nodes.description: Concurrency, latency, caching and reliability.

nodes.title: Cost & Production Monitoring
nodes.description: Token cost control, monitoring and incident response.

nodes.title: Coding Agents
nodes.description: Software engineering agents and code assistants.

nodes.title: Browser & Computer-Use Agents
nodes.description: Web automation, GUI control and computer use.

nodes.title: Voice & Realtime Agents
nodes.description: Speech, realtime and multimodal agents.

nodes.title: Domain-Specific Agents
nodes.description: Research, data, customer support and RPA agents.

stages.summary: Open problems, research directions and continuous learning.
